commands.author: merlinrabens
commands.command: review accepted — you're right, the non-throwing string normalizer drops configured secretrefs and lets ambient env credentials win on the brave/ollama/voyage paths. that's a real fail-open regression vs the existing strict behavior. reworking now to follow the pattern @steipete and @joshavant established in #76987 + the existing telegram/feishu/firecrawl/xai inspect-mode resolvers: 1. switch each resolver (`readconfiguredsecretstring`, `resolvememorysecretinputstring`, ollama local resolver, skill env-overrides) to use `resolvesecretinputstring({mode: "inspect"})`. 2. return a rich `available | missing | configured_unavailable` status instead of `string | undefined`. 3. update each consumer to fail closed on `configured_unavailable` (no fallback to ambient env), keep `missing` as the fallback-eligible state. 4. for skill `apikey` env-overrides: skip env injection on `configured_unavailable` (don't throw, don't silently fall back, don't inject — caller subprocess sees an unauth state and can decide). 5. add regression tests per file (matching the acceptance criteria you listed). 6. add changelog entry. the codex catalog change (400k context + 272k input cap) is independent and has no security surface — keeping that as-is. will force-push when the rework + tests pass locally. closing the security-sensitive gap is the priority over staying small.
commands.freeform_prompt: review accepted — you're right, the non-throwing string normalizer drops configured SecretRefs and lets ambient env credentials win on the brave/ollama/voyage paths. That's a real fail-open regression vs the existing strict behavior. Reworking now to follow the pattern @steipete and @joshavant established in #76987 + the existing telegram/feishu/firecrawl/xai inspect-mode resolvers: 1. Switch each resolver (`readConfiguredSecretString`, `resolveMemorySecretInputString`, ollama local resolver, skill env-overrides) to use `resolveSecretInputString({mode: "inspect"})`. 2. Return a rich `available | missing | configured_unavailable` status instead of `string | undefined`. 3. Update each consumer to fail closed on `configured_unavailable` (no fallback to ambient env), keep `missing` as the fallback-eligible state. 4. For skill `apiKey` env-overrides: skip env injection on `configured_unavailable` (don't throw, don't silently fall back, don't inject — caller subprocess sees an unauth state and can decide). 5. Add regression tests per file (matching the acceptance criteria you listed). 6. Add CHANGELOG entry. The codex catalog change (400K context + 272K input cap) is independent and has no security surface — keeping that as-is. Will force-push when the rework + tests pass locally. Closing the security-sensitive gap is the priority over staying small.
